changes.value:
## What
Add a `ValidatorTool` marker trait (like `AgentTool`) and a `/mcp/validator` endpoint on the existing MCP server that serves only validator-tagged tools.

No new server process. No new port. Just a second axum route on the same HTTP server.

**Files:**
- `swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tool_registry.rs` — add `ValidatorTool` trait, `is_validator_tool()` method, `retain_validator_tools()` on registry
- `swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/unified_server.rs` — add `/mcp/validator` route with filtered registry
- `swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/server.rs` — add `McpServer::new_validator(work_dir)` that registers all then retains only validator tools
- `swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/code_context/mod.rs` — mark as `ValidatorTool`
- `swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/files/mod.rs` — mark `FilesTool::read_only()` as `ValidatorTool`

**Approach:**
1. Add `fn is_validator_tool(&self) -> bool { false }` to `McpTool` trait
2. Add `pub trait ValidatorTool: McpTool {}` marker
3. Add `pub fn retain_validator_tools(&mut self)` to `ToolRegistry`
4. Tag `CodeContextTool` and `FilesTool` (read_only variant) with `ValidatorTool`
5. In `create_mcp_router()`, create a second `StreamableHttpService` with a validator-filtered `McpServer`
6. Mount at `/mcp/validator` alongside existing `/mcp`

Validator agent config URL: `http://localhost:{port}/mcp/validator`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `/mcp` serves full tool list (unchanged behavior)
- [ ] `/mcp/validator` serves only code_context + files (read-only)
- [ ] `is_validator_tool()` defaults to false
- [ ] `retain_validator_tools()` keeps only matching tools
- [ ] `/health` still works

## Tests
- [ ] Unit test: retain_validator_tools keeps only tagged tools
- [ ] Unit test: default is_validator_tool returns false
- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-tools`
